title: Chapter review manifest
description: Partition of a branch diff into logical review chapters. Written by the chapter-review skill to <git-dir>/chapter-review/chapters.json (tool state inside .git, keeping the worktree clean), consumed by the chapter-review VSCode extension. Structural rules only; the partition rules (every hunk in exactly one chapter or unassigned, no overlaps) are enforced by validate.mjs alongside this file, which is authoritative if the two disagree.

version.description: Contract version. Consumers must reject manifests with a version they don't know.

base.description: Ref the branch is reviewed against, usually the default branch.

mergeBase.description: git merge-base <base> <head> at generation time. The diff that was partitioned is mergeBase..head.

headSha.description: Commit head resolved to at generation time. Hunk coordinates refer to mergeBase..headSha; consumers use it to reconstruct exact content after the branch moves. Optional but strongly recommended.

summary.description: One or two sentences on what the branch does overall. Optional.

unassigned.description: Quarantined noise: lockfiles, snapshots, generated code, pure autoformat hunks. Always present, may be empty.

id.description: Positional ID, stable within one generation only.

description.description: Only when the why isn't obvious from the title.

oldPath.description: Pre-rename path. Only meaningful with status "renamed".

hunks.description: Omitted = the file's entire diff belongs to this entry (typical for added/deleted files). Present = only these hunks belong here; the file may then appear in other chapters with disjoint hunks.

note.description: Role of this file in this chapter. Only when the file appears in multiple chapters.

hunks.description: Omitted = whole file is unassigned. Present = only these hunks (e.g. autoformat noise in an otherwise meaningful file).

path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path, no leading slash.

hunk.description: Coordinates from a unified diff @@ -oldStart,oldLines +newStart,newLines @@ header.
